This should only trigger once per day and should be focused with asking a user if they finished an item, and if not how much is left or did they throw it away, similar to the update item form. We want to streamline the item review/updating process. Implementation plan: AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md"}

{"description":"Add two schema changes required by the at-risk review feature and the push notification reminder, then run a Prisma migration.

FILE: prisma/schema.prisma

CHANGE 1 — NotificationType enum
Add REVIEW_REMINDER to the existing enum so the daily cron can record that a reminder was sent and the dedup query can filter on it:

  enum NotificationType {
    ITEM_EXPIRING
    ITEM_EXPIRED
    ITEM_STATUS_CHANGED
    REVIEW_REMINDER   // new
    SYSTEM
  }

CHANGE 2 — User model
Add a nullable lastReviewedAt DateTime field to the User model (after the existing 'onboarded' field, before the closing @@unique):

  model User {
    ...
    onboarded Boolean @default(false)
    lastReviewedAt DateTime?   // new
    ...
  }

FILE: src/types/user-preferences.ts

CHANGE 3 — userPreferencesSchema
Add two new preference keys inside the userPreferencesSchema z.object({...}) block, below the existing emailFrequency field:

  reviewReminderEnabled: z.boolean().default(true),
  reviewReminderTime: z.string().regex(/^\d{2}:\d{2}$/).default('09:00'),

The reviewReminderTime field is added now so the schema is stable, but the time-picker UI is out of scope for this issue. The reviewReminderEnabled key is used by the Settings UI (broccoli-dashboard-7kc) and the cron script (broccoli-dashboard-gcy).

IMPORTANT: The userPreferencesSchema uses a dynamic pattern — AI provider fields are generated via a loop at the top of the file. The new fields are static and should be added to the z.object({}) literal below the existing emailFrequency field. Because the schema drives PreferenceKey (via keyof), UserSettingSwitchCard will automatically accept 'reviewReminderEnabled' as a valid settingKey after this change.

MIGRATION
Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name add-review-reminder-schema
Verify the generated migration file is committed to git. The migration will add one column to the users table and one value to the NotificationType enum. No data backfill is needed."}

{"id":"broccoli-dashboard-0cr","title":"Schema changes: REVIEW_REMINDER notification type + lastReviewedAt on User","description":"Add two schema changes required by the at-risk review feature and the push notification reminder, then run a Prisma migration.

CHANGE 1 — NotificationType enum (prisma/schema.prisma)
Add REVIEW_REMINDER to the existing enum so the daily cron can record that a reminder was sent and the dedup query can filter on it:

  enum NotificationType {
    ITEM_EXPIRING
    ITEM_EXPIRED
    ITEM_STATUS_CHANGED
    REVIEW_REMINDER   // new
    SYSTEM
  }

CHANGE 2 — User model (prisma/schema.prisma)
Add a nullable lastReviewedAt DateTime field so the server can track when a user last completed a review session. This is the cross-device source of truth; localStorage is only a fast-path cache:

  model User {
    ...
    lastReviewedAt DateTime?
    ...
  }

CHANGE 3 — userPreferencesSchema (src/types/user-preferences.ts)
Add two new preference keys used by the notification settings UI and the cron:

  reviewReminderEnabled: z.boolean().default(true),
  reviewReminderTime: z.string().regex(/^\d{2}:\d{2}$/).default('09:00'),

The reviewReminderTime field is added now so the schema is stable, but the time-picker UI is out of scope for this issue — just the schema addition and default.

MIGRATION
Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name add-review-reminder-schema
Verify migration file is generated and committed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:06:34Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:06:34Z"}

{"description":"Create the two client-side hooks that power the review session logic. Both hooks live in src/components/core/AtRiskReview/ (create this directory — it does not exist yet).

--- HOOK 1: useAtRiskReview.ts ---

TYPES (define at top of file, also exported for use by other components):

  import type { ItemStatusType } from '@/generated/prisma/client';
  import type { CombinedItemType } from '@/components/core/Inventory';

  export type ReviewDecision =
    | { type: 'eaten' }
    | { type: 'discarded' }
    | { type: 'updated'; percentConsumed: number; status?: ItemStatusType }
    | { type: 'skipped' };

  export interface ReviewResult {
    itemId: number;
    decision: ReviewDecision;
  }

RESPONSIBILITIES:
- Calls api.item.getAtRiskItems.useQuery() (added in broccoli-dashboard-k5p).
- Filters results client-side: include BAD and OLD items always; include FRESH items only if getDaysUntilExpiration(item) <= 3. Import getDaysUntilExpiration from '@/utils/expiration'.
- Initialises queue: CombinedItemType[] from the filtered result (only set once on first load — do NOT reset queue on every query refetch, or cards in progress would disappear).
- Maintains results: ReviewResult[] (decisions taken so far).
- Exposes advance(itemId: number, decision: ReviewDecision): removes the item from the queue, appends to results, fires the correct tRPC mutation.

CRITICAL — updateItem mutation constraint:
The existing api.item.updateItem mutation (src/types/item.ts) requires ALL fields: id, name, price, quantity, groceryTripId, importId, itemTypes, percentConsumed, status. It is NOT a partial update. When advance() fires for 'eaten' or 'discarded' or 'updated', you must spread the full item fields:

  api.item.updateItem.useMutation({...}).mutate({
    id: item.id,
    name: item.name,
    description: item.description ?? '',
    price: String(item.price),
    quantity: item.quantity,
    unit: item.unit ?? '',
    groceryTripId: item.groceryTripId,
    importId: item.importId ?? '',
    itemTypes: item.itemTypes.map(t => ({ id: t.id })),
    percentConsumed: decision.type === 'eaten' ? 100 : (decision.type === 'updated' ? decision.percentConsumed : item.percentConsumed),
    status: decision.type === 'eaten' ? 'EATEN' : decision.type === 'discarded' ? 'DISCARDED' : (decision.type === 'updated' && decision.status ? decision.status : item.status),
  });

For 'skipped': no mutation, just pop the queue.

On mutation success: call utils.item.getItems.invalidate() so the Inventory list (which uses getItems) stays in sync. Do NOT invalidate getAtRiskItems — the queue is managed in local state.

RETURNED VALUES:
  {
    queue: CombinedItemType[],       // items not yet decided
    results: ReviewResult[],          // completed decisions
    currentItem: CombinedItemType | undefined,  // queue[0]
    advance: (itemId: number, decision: ReviewDecision) => void,
    isLoading: boolean,               // query is pending
    isEmpty: boolean,                 // queue.length === 0 (and not loading)
    eatenCount: number,
    discardedCount: number,
    skippedCount: number,
  }

--- HOOK 2: useAtRiskReviewSession.ts ---

CONSTANTS:
  const STORAGE_KEY = 'broccoli:atRiskReview:lastCompletedDate';

LOGIC:
  shouldShowReview(): boolean
    - Reads localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY)
    - Returns true if null/empty OR if the stored date string does not match new Date().toDateString()
    - Returns false if the stored date matches today (already reviewed today on this device)

  markComplete(): void
    - Writes new Date().toISOString() to localStorage under STORAGE_KEY
    - Also calls api.user.markReviewComplete.mutate() to persist cross-device

  forceOpen state: boolean
    - useState(false), exposed as { forceOpen, setForceOpen }
    - When true, bypasses the shouldShowReview() gate (used by the push notification deep-link)

RETURNED VALUES:
  {
    shouldShow: boolean,         // shouldShowReview() || forceOpen
    markComplete: () => void,
    forceOpen: boolean,
    setForceOpen: (v: boolean) => void,
  }

DEPENDS ON: broccoli-dashboard-k5p (for api.item.getAtRiskItems and api.user.markReviewComplete)."}

{"id":"broccoli-dashboard-z4y","title":"AtRiskReviewCard: draggable card with Framer Motion","description":"Build the core draggable card component at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewCard.tsx. This is the most complex component in the feature — get the physics and visual feedback right.

PROPS:
  interface AtRiskReviewCardProps {
    item: CombinedItemType;
    onDecision: (decision: ReviewDecision) => void;
    style?: MotionStyle;  // for deck positioning (scale, translateY, zIndex passed from AtRiskReviewDeck)
    isTop: boolean;       // only the top card is draggable
  }

FRAMER MOTION SETUP:
  const dragX = useMotionValue(0);
  const dragY = useMotionValue(0);
  const rotate = useTransform(dragX, [-200, 0, 200], [-15, 0, 15]);
  const SWIPE_THRESHOLD = 80;

  Drag config:
  - drag={isTop ? true : false}
  - dragConstraints={{ left: 0, right: 0, top: -100, bottom: 0 }} (elastic feel — card snaps back if below threshold)
  - dragElastic={0.7}
  - whileDrag={{ cursor: 'grabbing' }}

  handleDragEnd(_: PointerEvent, info: PanInfo):
  - if info.offset.x > SWIPE_THRESHOLD → onDecision({ type: 'eaten' })
  - if info.offset.x < -SWIPE_THRESHOLD → onDecision({ type: 'discarded' })
  - if info.offset.y < -SWIPE_THRESHOLD → onDecision({ type: 'skipped' })
  - else → animate back to { x: 0, y: 0, rotate: 0 } via spring

  On programmatic decision (button taps), animate the card off-screen first via animate(), THEN call onDecision in the animation completion callback so the parent doesn't remove it before the exit animation finishes.

CARD VISUAL STRUCTURE (Tailwind):
- Outer wrapper: rounded-2xl shadow-xl bg-white dark:bg-zinc-900 overflow-hidden w-full max-w-sm mx-auto
- Status header strip: colored top border (4px) and subtle tinted background:
    BAD → border-red-400, bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/30
    OLD → border-yellow-400, bg-yellow-50 dark:bg-yellow-950/30
    FRESH expiring → border-orange-400, bg-orange-50 dark:bg-orange-950/30
- Status badge + urgency string (top-left): reuse getItemStatusColor from ItemForm.tsx
- Urgency copy (top-right, muted):
    BAD → 'This has gone bad'
    OLD → 'Going bad soon'
    FRESH 0 days → 'Expires today!'
    FRESH 1 day → 'Expires tomorrow'
    FRESH 2-3 days → 'Expires in N days'
    Use getTimeRemainingString from src/utils/expiration.ts
- Item type icon: centered, large (64x64) using existing ImageFallback component with urlEndpoint and fallback
- Item name: text-2xl font-bold text-center
- Item details: text-sm text-muted-foreground text-center (description or 'quantity unit')
- % consumed bar: if percentConsumed > 0, show a simple progress bar with label
- Action buttons row: two large buttons — Discarded (left, red outline) and Eaten (right, green fill)
- 'Update details' chevron button at bottom center that toggles AtRiskReviewCardDetails (passed as children or via isExpanded state)

REDUCED MOTION: wrap rotation and fly-out animation in a check for window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') — if true, skip rotate transform and use opacity fade instead of position animation.

DEPENDS ON: useAtRiskReview hook (broccoli-dashboard-479).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:07:24Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:07:24Z"}

{"id":"broccoli-dashboard-rqy","title":"AtRiskReviewCardOverlay: directional swipe hint overlays","description":"Build the directional action hint overlays that appear on the card as the user drags. Lives at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewCardOverlay.tsx. This component is rendered inside AtRiskReviewCard and receives the dragX MotionValue as a prop so it can derive its own opacity reactively without re-renders.

PROPS:
  interface AtRiskReviewCardOverlayProps {
    dragX: MotionValue<number>;
    threshold: number;  // SWIPE_THRESHOLD constant from parent (80)
  }

IMPLEMENTATION:
  Two absolutely-positioned overlays, both covering the full card face (inset-0 absolute pointer-events-none rounded-2xl):

  RIGHT overlay (Eaten):
    const eatOpacity = useTransform(dragX, [0, threshold], [0, 1]);
    Background: bg-green-500/20 dark:bg-green-400/20
    Content: centered ✅ icon (Lucide Utensils, size 48) + bold 'EATEN' label
    Text color: text-green-700 dark:text-green-300
    Border: border-2 border-green-500/50 rounded-2xl

  LEFT overlay (Discard):
    const discardOpacity = useTransform(dragX, [-threshold, 0], [1, 0]);
    Background: bg-red-500/20 dark:bg-red-400/20
    Content: centered 🗑 icon (Lucide Trash2, size 48) + bold 'DISCARD' label
    Text color: text-red-700 dark:text-red-300
    Border: border-2 border-red-500/50 rounded-2xl

Both overlays use <motion.div style={{ opacity }} ...> so opacity is driven by the MotionValue directly (no React state, no re-renders). The overlays should be z-10 relative to card content so they render above the card body but below any absolutely-positioned buttons.

DESIGN NOTE: The opacity ramp starts at 0 and reaches 1 exactly at the SWIPE_THRESHOLD (80px). This gives a clear signal that the action will commit. Beyond threshold, opacity stays at 1 (useTransform clamps by default).

DEPENDS ON: AtRiskReviewCard (broccoli-dashboard-z4y).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:07:39Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:07:39Z"}

{"id":"broccoli-dashboard-le3","title":"AtRiskReviewCardDetails: expandable inline % consumed + status panel","description":"Build the expandable detail panel that slides open when the user taps 'Update details' on a card. Lives at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewCardDetails.tsx. This replaces having to open the full UpdateItemForm dialog — it brings the most important controls inline on the card.

PROPS:
  interface AtRiskReviewCardDetailsProps {
    item: CombinedItemType;
    onConfirm: (decision: { type: 'updated'; percentConsumed: number; status?: ItemStatusType }) => void;
    onCancel: () => void;
  }

LOCAL STATE:
  - percentConsumed: number (initialized from item.percentConsumed)
  - status: ItemStatusType | undefined (initialized as undefined — 'in progress')

ANIMATION: Wrap with <motion.div layout animate={{ height: 'auto' }} initial={{ height: 0 }} exit={{ height: 0 }} transition={{ type: 'spring', bounce: 0.1 }}> inside an AnimatePresence block in the parent card.

UI LAYOUT (mirrors UpdateItemForm design — reuse its visual patterns):
  1. Large percentage display: centered, text-5xl font-bold tabular-nums showing '{percentConsumed}%'
  2. Native range slider: input type=range 0-100, styled with accent-primary
  3. Quick-pick buttons grid (5 columns): [0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%] — highlighted when selected (variant='default' vs 'outline')
  4. Status toggle row: two large buttons side-by-side
       [🍽 Still eating]  [✅ All eaten]  [🗑 Threw it out]
     - 'Still eating' clears status override (stays as item's current status with updated %)
     - 'All eaten' sets status=EATEN and percentConsumed=100
     - 'Threw it out' sets status=DISCARDED
  5. Confirm button: full-width, green, 'Save & Next →' — calls onConfirm with current state
  6. Cancel link: small text button 'Never mind' — calls onCancel to collapse the panel

SMART DEFAULTS: If percentConsumed reaches 100 via the slider or quick-pick buttons, automatically highlight 'All eaten'. If status is set to DISCARDED, the % is visually irrelevant (grey it out).

REUSE: The % display and quick-pick button pattern are already implemented in UpdateItemForm — copy the JSX pattern from src/components/core/ItemForm.tsx UpdateItemForm component.

DEPENDS ON: useAtRiskReview hook (broccoli-dashboard-479), should be built alongside AtRiskReviewCard (broccoli-dashboard-z4y).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:07:57Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:07:57Z"}

{"id":"broccoli-dashboard-8d4","title":"AtRiskReviewDeck: stacked card deck orchestrator","description":"Build the deck component that renders the stacked card queue and manages AnimatePresence transitions between cards. Lives at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewDeck.tsx.

PROPS:
  interface AtRiskReviewDeckProps {
    queue: CombinedItemType[];
    onDecision: (itemId: number, decision: ReviewDecision) => void;
  }

STACKING LAYOUT:
Render the top 3 items from queue simultaneously (or fewer if queue.length < 3), each as an absolutely-positioned AtRiskReviewCard. The container div needs position: relative with a fixed height tall enough for the largest possible card (use min-h-[520px] or similar).

Positioning per stack depth (index 0 = top/front):
  index 0 (top):    scale=1.00, translateY=0px,  zIndex=3, isTop=true
  index 1 (middle): scale=0.96, translateY=8px,  zIndex=2, isTop=false
  index 2 (back):   scale=0.92, translateY=16px, zIndex=1, isTop=false

Pass these as initial/animate props on each card's wrapper motion.div so they animate smoothly when the top card is removed and the stack 'shuffles up'.

ANIMATEPRESENCE:
Wrap the card list in <AnimatePresence mode='popLayout'> keyed by item.id. When the top card is removed from queue (after onDecision fires), AnimatePresence handles the exit animation (card already flew off-screen via AtRiskReviewCard's own animation) and the remaining cards animate into their new depth positions via their layout prop.

EMPTY STATE: When queue.length === 0, render null (the parent AtRiskReviewModal will switch to the AtRiskReviewComplete screen).

SKELETON STATE: When isLoading is true (passed as prop), render a single skeleton card placeholder — a rounded-2xl with animate-pulse bg-muted at the same dimensions as a card.

GESTURE HINT: On first render (or if no swipe has been detected after 2 seconds), show a subtle animated hint — a small arrow/swipe icon that animates left and right to guide the user. Dismiss permanently once first swipe is detected. Store dismissal in localStorage key 'broccoli:atRiskReview:hintSeen'.

DEPENDS ON: AtRiskReviewCard (broccoli-dashboard-z4y), AtRiskReviewCardOverlay (broccoli-dashboard-rqy), AtRiskReviewCardDetails (broccoli-dashboard-le3).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:08:14Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:08:14Z"}

{"id":"broccoli-dashboard-n5z","title":"AtRiskReviewModal: Vaul Drawer shell with once-per-day gate + progress bar","description":"Build the top-level modal shell that wraps the card deck experience. Lives at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewModal.tsx. This is the entry point component mounted in AppPage.

RESPONSIBILITIES:
1. Gate logic — uses useAtRiskReviewSession to determine if the modal should auto-open. Only opens when:
   a. isMobile === true (from useMediaQueries context)
   b. session.shouldShow === true (not reviewed today, or forceOpen is true)
   c. atRiskItems.length > 0 (from useAtRiskReview)
   If any condition fails, renders null.

2. Modal shell — wraps content in the existing BottomDrawer component (src/components/BottomDrawer.tsx) with renderHeader={null} (no default header — custom header is inlined). Alternatively, use Vaul Drawer.Root directly for more control. The drawer should be full-height on mobile (h-[100dvh]) so the deck has maximum vertical space.

3. Custom header (rendered inside drawer):
   - Left: 'Kitchen check-in' title + item count subtitle ('N items need attention')
   - Right: X icon button with tooltip 'Remind me later' — closes modal WITHOUT marking session complete (user will see it again)
   - Below header: 'Don't show today' small text link — closes AND calls session.markComplete()

4. Progress bar: thin (h-1) bar below the header showing reviewed/total ratio. Use the existing @radix-ui/react-progress (Progress component). Example: 3 of 7 reviewed → 43% filled. Animate width transitions smoothly.

5. Body: renders AtRiskReviewDeck (passing queue, onDecision, isLoading) when queue is non-empty, or AtRiskReviewComplete when queue is empty and results exist.

6. Push notification deep-link handling:
   - Read ?intent=review query param via useQueryState('intent') from nuqs
   - If intent === 'review': call session.setForceOpen(true), then clear the param: router.replace('/app', { scroll: false })
   - Add a useEffect listener on navigator.serviceWorker for message events: if event.data?.type === 'OPEN_AT_RISK_REVIEW', call session.setForceOpen(true)
   - Both paths force the modal open regardless of the localStorage gate

7. On modal close (Drawer onOpenChange to false):
   - If queue is exhausted (isEmpty): call session.markComplete()
   - If dismissed mid-deck: do NOT call markComplete — just close

VAUL DRAWER CONFIG:
  <Drawer.Root open={isOpen} onOpenChange={handleOpenChange}>
    Use the existing BottomDrawer or replicate its pattern. Set snapPoints if needed for full-height feel on mobile. The drawer should push/scale the background content slightly (existing BottomDrawer already does this with the framer-motion scale animation).

DEPENDS ON: AtRiskReviewDeck (broccoli-dashboard-8d4), useAtRiskReviewSession (broccoli-dashboard-479), AtRiskReviewComplete (built in same phase — coordinate).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:08:38Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:08:38Z"}

{"id":"broccoli-dashboard-cjo","title":"AppPage integration: mount AtRiskReviewModal + accessibility + haptics","description":"Wire the completed AtRiskReview feature into the app and add the final polish layer. This is the last front-end task. FILE: src/app/(app)/(scrollable)/app/page.tsx Mount AtRiskReviewModal as the first child of the page, before the header div. It self-manages its open/close state so no props are needed: import { AtRiskReviewModal } from '@/components/core/AtRiskReview'; // Inside AppPage return:
... existing page content ...
Also add index.ts at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/index.ts re-exporting all public components and hooks. ACCESSIBILITY: - Add keyboard navigation to AtRiskReviewDeck: useHotkeys (react-hotkeys-hook, already installed) binding ArrowRight → onDecision('eaten'), ArrowLeft → onDecision('discarded'), ArrowUp → onDecision('skipped'), Escape → close modal. Only active when the deck is open and isTop card is mounted. - All card interactive elements (drag area, buttons) must have aria-label attributes. Drag area: aria-label='Drag card: swipe right if eaten, left to discard'. Eaten button: aria-label='Mark as eaten'. Discard button: aria-label='Mark as discarded'. - AtRiskReviewModal Drawer.Title should read 'Kitchen check-in: review {count} at-risk items'. - Focus management: when modal opens, focus the first action button on the top card. When modal closes, return focus to the element that was focused before. HAPTIC FEEDBACK: - In AtRiskReviewCard's handleDragEnd, after determining the decision type and BEFORE calling onDecision, call: if ('vibrate' in navigator) navigator.vibrate(50); - For button taps (Eaten/Discard buttons), same pattern — vibrate on click. - Do not vibrate for 'skipped' — that is intentional and passive. DARK MODE REVIEW: - Walk through all status colors in both themes. Confirm: - Red-400 border + red-950/30 bg works in dark - Yellow-400 border + yellow-950/30 bg works in dark - Orange-400 border + orange-950/30 bg works in dark - Green/Red overlays are visible but not garish in dark mode - The Vaul Drawer uses dark:bg-zinc-900 (consistent with existing BottomDrawer) EDGE CASES TO VERIFY: - 0 items: modal never mounts (gate in useAtRiskReview ensures isEmpty=true from the start) - 1 item: single card with no stack effect (AtRiskReviewDeck handles queue.length < 3 gracefully) - Slow network: skeleton card shows while query is loading - User closes mid-deck and reopens app same day: modal re-opens with remaining items (localStorage not marked complete, session not marked complete) - User closes mid-deck and reopens app next day: modal opens fresh with full new at-risk list DEPENDS ON: AtRiskReviewModal (broccoli-dashboard-n5z), AtRiskReviewComplete (broccoli-dashboard-ehp).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:09:15Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:09:15Z"}

{"description":"Add a 'Daily Review Reminder' sub-section to SettingsTabNotifications so users can opt out of the daily push nudge.

FILE: src/components/settings/SettingsTabNotifications.tsx

CHANGE: Add a new Card below the existing Push Notifications Card. Only render it when isSubscribed === true (no point showing reminder settings if push is disabled — the cron already skips users without push subscriptions).

The existing component already imports Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent from '@/components/ui/card', Bell from lucide-react, and UserSettingSwitchCard. You only need to add the CalendarClock import from lucide-react.

ADD this Card as the last child inside the outer div.space-y-4:

  {isSubscribed && (
    <Card>
      <CardHeader>
        <CardTitle className='flex items-center gap-2'>
          <CalendarClock className='h-5 w-5' />
          Daily Review Reminder
        </CardTitle>
        <CardDescription>
          Get a daily nudge when you have items that need attention.
          Sent once per day at 9 AM.
        </CardDescription>
      </CardHeader>
      <CardContent>
        <UserSettingSwitchCard
          settingKey='reviewReminderEnabled'
          label='Enable daily reminder'
          description='We will send you a push notification when you have BAD, OLD, or soon-to-expire items to review.'
        />
      </CardContent>
    </Card>
  )}

HOW UserSettingSwitchCard WIRES UP:
UserSettingSwitchCard (src/components/UserSettingSwitchCard.tsx) reads and writes preferences via api.user.getSinglePreference and api.user.updatePreference tRPC calls. It derives its type-safety from PreferenceKey, which is keyof UserPreferences, which is derived from userPreferencesSchema. Once reviewReminderEnabled is added to that schema (broccoli-dashboard-0cr), passing settingKey='reviewReminderEnabled' here is type-safe with no additional wiring needed.

FUTURE NOTE: Add a comment below the UserSettingSwitchCard:
  // TODO: Add time picker for reviewReminderTime preference once UI pattern is decided.

DEPENDENCY CHAIN VERIFICATION:
- broccoli-dashboard-0cr adds reviewReminderEnabled to userPreferencesSchema
- That makes 'reviewReminderEnabled' a valid PreferenceKey

{"id":"broccoli-dashboard-0cr","title":"Schema changes: REVIEW_REMINDER notification type + lastReviewedAt on User","description":"Add two schema changes required by the at-risk review feature and the push notification reminder, then run a Prisma migration.

FILE: prisma/schema.prisma

CHANGE 1 — NotificationType enum
Add REVIEW_REMINDER to the existing enum so the daily cron can record that a reminder was sent and the dedup query can filter on it:

  enum NotificationType {
    ITEM_EXPIRING
    ITEM_EXPIRED
    ITEM_STATUS_CHANGED
    REVIEW_REMINDER   // new
    SYSTEM
  }

CHANGE 2 — User model
Add a nullable lastReviewedAt DateTime field to the User model (after the existing 'onboarded' field, before the closing @@unique):

  model User {
    ...
    onboarded Boolean @default(false)
    lastReviewedAt DateTime?   // new
    ...
  }

FILE: src/types/user-preferences.ts

CHANGE 3 — userPreferencesSchema
Add two new preference keys inside the userPreferencesSchema z.object({...}) block, below the existing emailFrequency field:

  reviewReminderEnabled: z.boolean().default(true),
  reviewReminderTime: z.string().regex(/^\d{2}:\d{2}$/).default('09:00'),

The reviewReminderTime field is added now so the schema is stable, but the time-picker UI is out of scope for this issue. The reviewReminderEnabled key is used by the Settings UI (broccoli-dashboard-7kc) and the cron script (broccoli-dashboard-gcy).

IMPORTANT: The userPreferencesSchema uses a dynamic pattern — AI provider fields are generated via a loop at the top of the file. The new fields are static and should be added to the z.object({}) literal below the existing emailFrequency field. Because the schema drives PreferenceKey (via keyof), UserSettingSwitchCard will automatically accept 'reviewReminderEnabled' as a valid settingKey after this change.

MIGRATION
Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name add-review-reminder-schema
Verify the generated migration file is committed to git. The migration will add one column to the users table and one value to the NotificationType enum. No data backfill is needed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:06:34Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:16:55Z"}

WORK REQUIRED:

2. Wire it to the reviewReminderTime preference key via api.user.updatePreference.

{"description":"After a card is swiped, show a floating 'Undo' toast via sonner (already installed) for 3 seconds. If the user taps Undo before it disappears, reverse the last decision: remove the result from review.results, push the item back to the front of the queue, and fire a compensating updateItem mutation to restore its previous status/percentConsumed.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- In useAtRiskReview, expose an undo() function alongside advance(). undo() only works if results.length > 0.
- The toast should be triggered from AtRiskReviewDeck after onDecision fires successfully. Use sonner's toast() with an action button.
- Only support undoing the MOST RECENT decision (single-level undo). Calling undo twice in a row would undo the same action twice -- guard against this.
- 'skipped' decisions should also be undoable (puts item back at front of queue, no mutation needed).
- The compensating mutation for 'eaten' should restore percentConsumed and status to the item's original values (captured at queue-init time in useAtRiskReview).

DEPENDS ON: broccoli-dashboard-8d4 (AtRiskReviewDeck must exist), broccoli-dashboard-479 (useAtRiskReview hook must exist)."}

old_value:
{"id":"broccoli-dashboard-479","title":"useAtRiskReview + useAtRiskReviewSession hooks","description":"Create the two client-side hooks that power the review session logic. Both hooks live in src/components/core/AtRiskReview/ (create this directory — it does not exist yet).

--- HOOK 1: useAtRiskReview.ts ---

TYPES (define at top of file, also exported for use by other components):

  import type { ItemStatusType } from '@/generated/prisma/client';
  import type { CombinedItemType } from '@/components/core/Inventory';

  export type ReviewDecision =
    | { type: 'eaten' }
    | { type: 'discarded' }
    | { type: 'updated'; percentConsumed: number; status?: ItemStatusType }
    | { type: 'skipped' };

  export interface ReviewResult {
    itemId: number;
    decision: ReviewDecision;
  }

RESPONSIBILITIES:
- Calls api.item.getAtRiskItems.useQuery() (added in broccoli-dashboard-k5p).
- Filters results client-side: include BAD and OLD items always; include FRESH items only if getDaysUntilExpiration(item) <= 3. Import getDaysUntilExpiration from '@/utils/expiration'.
- Initialises queue: CombinedItemType[] from the filtered result (only set once on first load — do NOT reset queue on every query refetch, or cards in progress would disappear).
- Maintains results: ReviewResult[] (decisions taken so far).
- Exposes advance(itemId: number, decision: ReviewDecision): removes the item from the queue, appends to results, fires the correct tRPC mutation.

CRITICAL — updateItem mutation constraint:
The existing api.item.updateItem mutation (src/types/item.ts) requires ALL fields: id, name, price, quantity, groceryTripId, importId, itemTypes, percentConsumed, status. It is NOT a partial update. When advance() fires for 'eaten' or 'discarded' or 'updated', you must spread the full item fields:

  api.item.updateItem.useMutation({...}).mutate({
    id: item.id,
    name: item.name,
    description: item.description ?? '',
    price: String(item.price),
    quantity: item.quantity,
    unit: item.unit ?? '',
    groceryTripId: item.groceryTripId,
    importId: item.importId ?? '',
    itemTypes: item.itemTypes.map(t => ({ id: t.id })),
    percentConsumed: decision.type === 'eaten' ? 100 : (decision.type === 'updated' ? decision.percentConsumed : item.percentConsumed),
    status: decision.type === 'eaten' ? 'EATEN' : decision.type === 'discarded' ? 'DISCARDED' : (decision.type === 'updated' && decision.status ? decision.status : item.status),
  });

For 'skipped': no mutation, just pop the queue.

On mutation success: call utils.item.getItems.invalidate() so the Inventory list (which uses getItems) stays in sync. Do NOT invalidate getAtRiskItems — the queue is managed in local state.

RETURNED VALUES:
  {
    queue: CombinedItemType[],       // items not yet decided
    results: ReviewResult[],          // completed decisions
    currentItem: CombinedItemType | undefined,  // queue[0]
    advance: (itemId: number, decision: ReviewDecision) => void,
    isLoading: boolean,               // query is pending
    isEmpty: boolean,                 // queue.length === 0 (and not loading)
    eatenCount: number,
    discardedCount: number,
    skippedCount: number,
  }

--- HOOK 2: useAtRiskReviewSession.ts ---

CONSTANTS:
  const STORAGE_KEY = 'broccoli:atRiskReview:lastCompletedDate';

LOGIC:
  shouldShowReview(): boolean
    - Reads localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY)
    - Returns true if null/empty OR if the stored date string does not match new Date().toDateString()
    - Returns false if the stored date matches today (already reviewed today on this device)

  markComplete(): void
    - Writes new Date().toISOString() to localStorage under STORAGE_KEY
    - Also calls api.user.markReviewComplete.mutate() to persist cross-device

  forceOpen state: boolean
    - useState(false), exposed as { forceOpen, setForceOpen }
    - When true, bypasses the shouldShowReview() gate (used by the push notification deep-link)

RETURNED VALUES:
  {
    shouldShow: boolean,         // shouldShowReview() || forceOpen
    markComplete: () => void,
    forceOpen: boolean,
    setForceOpen: (v: boolean) => void,
  }

DEPENDS ON: broccoli-dashboard-k5p (for api.item.getAtRiskItems and api.user.markReviewComplete).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:07:03Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:17:35Z"}

new_value: Implemented both hooks. useAtRiskReview: queue initialised once from getAtRiskItems query, advance() fires full-object updateItem mutation, invalidates getItems on success, exposes queue/currentItem/results/counts. useAtRiskReviewSession: localStorage once-per-day gate, markComplete writes localStorage + calls markReviewComplete mutation, forceOpen bypass for push deep-link. Both pass tsc --noEmit.

{"description":"Two inconsistencies between AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md and the actual implementation. Both are already correct in code — the plan doc needs updating.

INCONSISTENCY 1 — markComplete localStorage format (line 156):
  Plan says: localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, new Date().toISOString())
  Impl uses:  localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, new Date().toDateString())
  Why it matters: shouldShowReview() compares stored !== new Date().toDateString(). Storing toISOString() would never match toDateString(), so the once-per-day gate would never engage. The implementation is correct; the plan is wrong.
  Fix: Change line 156 to: localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, new Date().toDateString())

INCONSISTENCY 2 — ReviewDecision 'eaten' variant (lines 169-173):
  Plan says: { type: 'eaten'; percentConsumed: 100 }
  Impl uses:  { type: 'eaten' }  (no percentConsumed field — it's inferred as 100 inside advance())
  Why it matters: AtRiskReviewCard.tsx onDecision({ type: 'eaten' }) will typecheck against the hook's actual type, not the plan's. If someone reads the plan and adds percentConsumed to a call site, it will cause a TS error.
  Fix: Update plan lines 169-173 to match the implemented type union.

ACTION: Update AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md lines 155-156 and 169-173 to match the implementation. No code changes needed."}
old_value: {"id":"broccoli-dashboard-3a3","title":"AtRiskReview: fix plan inconsistency — markComplete should store toDateString not toISOString","description":"The AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md spec for useAtRiskReviewSession.markComplete() says to write new Date().toISOString() to localStorage, but shouldShowReview() compares stored !== new Date().toDateString(). Comparing an ISO timestamp string against a dateString would always return true (they're different formats), meaning the once-per-day gate would never engage. The implementation in useAtRiskReviewSession.ts correctly stores toDateString() (both sides match). The plan doc itself is wrong and should be updated if it's used as a reference for future work. No code change needed — just a plan doc correction.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T14:05:41Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:05:41Z"}

INCONSISTENCY 1 — markComplete localStorage format (line 156):
  Plan says: localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, new Date().toISOString())
  Impl uses:  localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, new Date().toDateString())
  Why it matters: shouldShowReview() compares stored !== new Date().toDateString(). Storing toISOString() would never match toDateString(), so the once-per-day gate would never engage. The implementation is correct; the plan is wrong.
  Fix: Change line 156 to: localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, new Date().toDateString())

INCONSISTENCY 2 — ReviewDecision 'eaten' variant (lines 169-173):
  Plan says: { type: 'eaten'; percentConsumed: 100 }
  Impl uses:  { type: 'eaten' }  (no percentConsumed field — it's inferred as 100 inside advance())
  Why it matters: AtRiskReviewCard.tsx onDecision({ type: 'eaten' }) will typecheck against the hook's actual type, not the plan's. If someone reads the plan and adds percentConsumed to a call site, it will cause a TS error.

No code changes needed.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-09T14:05:41Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:27:21Z"}

{"description":"In send-review-reminders.ts (gcy spec), the sendPush call has data: { type: 'AT_RISK_REVIEW', tag: 'at-risk-review' }. The tag inside data is redundant. The notification tag is a top-level showNotification option; the service worker (a2b) computes it from data.data.type already. When implementing gcy, use data: { type: 'AT_RISK_REVIEW' } only — drop the tag key. Minor cleanup, avoids confusion when reading payloads in DevTools."}

{"description":"The at-risk review deck components are all built but the feature is NOT yet accessible to any user. This tracks the minimum path to a first working end-to-end experience.

WHAT IS BUILT (closed issues):
- broccoli-dashboard-479: useAtRiskReview + useAtRiskReviewSession hooks
- broccoli-dashboard-z4y: AtRiskReviewCard
- broccoli-dashboard-le3: AtRiskReviewCardDetails
- broccoli-dashboard-rqy: AtRiskReviewCardOverlay
- broccoli-dashboard-8d4: AtRiskReviewDeck
- broccoli-dashboard-0cr: Schema (REVIEW_REMINDER type + lastReviewedAt field)
- broccoli-dashboard-k5p: tRPC queries (getAtRiskItems + markReviewComplete)
- broccoli-dashboard-gcy: Cron script (send-review-reminders.ts)
- broccoli-dashboard-ehp: AtRiskReviewComplete (completion screen) ✅ DONE
- broccoli-dashboard-n5z: AtRiskReviewModal (Vaul drawer shell + once-per-day gate) ✅ DONE
  NOTE: AtRiskReviewModal already handles SW postMessage (OPEN_AT_RISK_REVIEW) and intent=review URL param internally.
- broccoli-dashboard-ymd: index.ts barrel export ✅ DONE (538529b)

WHAT IS MISSING (open - blocks shipping):
1. broccoli-dashboard-cjo: AppPage integration — mount <AtRiskReviewModal /> in page.tsx (Parts 1, 4, 5 only — Part 2 barrel done, Part 3 keyboard nav deferred to p9c)

The feature is 0% user-visible until cjo Part 1 lands. That is a single import + one JSX line in AppPage.

PUSH NOTIFICATION PATH (parallel, not required for local testing):
2. broccoli-dashboard-a2b: SW deep-link for AT_RISK_REVIEW notifications

TESTING WITHOUT PUSH: Once cjo lands, the modal can be manually triggered via localStorage manipulation or the intent=review URL param without needing the push notification path."}

{"description":"Wire the feature into the app page and verify edge cases. The barrel export (Part 2) is already done (broccoli-dashboard-ymd, 538529b). The SW postMessage listener is already inside AtRiskReviewModal — no separate wiring needed in AppPage. --- PART 1: Mount AtRiskReviewModal in AppPage --- [CRITICAL — this is the only thing blocking user-visibility] FILE: src/app/(app)/(scrollable)/app/page.tsx Add the import and mount the modal as the first child: import { AtRiskReviewModal } from '@/components/core/AtRiskReview'; export default function AppPage() { return (
{/* ... existing page content ... */}
); } --- PART 2: barrel export --- DONE (broccoli-dashboard-ymd, 538529b) --- --- PART 3: Keyboard navigation --- DEFERRED to broccoli-dashboard-p9c --- --- PART 4: Verify edge cases manually --- Test matrix (document findings as comments in AtRiskReviewModal if any workaround is needed): - 0 at-risk items: modal never mounts (isEmpty=true from start, gate fails) - 1 item: single card, no stack behind it, deck renders gracefully (queue.slice(0,3) with 1 item = 1 card) - Slow network: skeleton shows while query isLoading=true - User closes mid-deck same day: localStorage NOT marked, modal re-opens with remaining queue on next open - User closes mid-deck next day: modal opens fresh (localStorage date mismatch) - Push notification tap (app closed): ?intent=review opens /app, modal mounts, setForceOpen(true) - Push notification tap (app open): postMessage OPEN_AT_RISK_REVIEW received, modal opens in-place --- PART 5: Dark mode verification --- Walk through each status color in dark mode and verify: - border-red-400 + bg-red-950/30 renders clearly on dark:bg-zinc-900 card - border-yellow-400 + bg-yellow-950/30 renders clearly - border-orange-400 + bg-orange-950/30 renders clearly - Green (EATEN) overlay and Red (DISCARD) overlay are visible but not garish DEPENDS ON: broccoli-dashboard-n5z (AtRiskReviewModal) ✅, broccoli-dashboard-ehp (AtRiskReviewComplete) ✅, broccoli-dashboard-ymd (barrel export) ✅"}
